title: Profit Interest Agreement (LLC)
description: Awards profit interests in an LLC, giving recipients rights to future appreciation and distributions.
whatsCovered: Vesting and forfeiture terms upon member exit
Capital account allocations and tax filings
Adherence to the LLC Operating Agreement
whenToUse: Use this agreement to grant equity incentive awards in a startup organized as a partnership or LLC rather than a corporation.

fields.label: Company Office Address
fields.placeholder: e.g. 100 Innovators Blvd, San Francisco, CA

clauses.heading: PROFIT INTEREST AGREEMENT (LLC)
clauses.body: This startup agreement (the "Agreement") is entered into as of {effective_date} (the "Effective Date"), by and between {company_name}, located at {company_address} (the "Company"), and the counterparty or stakeholders executing this document below.

clauses.heading: 1. Core Agreement Terms
clauses.body: This agreement sets out the transaction and operational terms for Profit Interest Agreement (LLC). The parties agree to coordinate in good faith to complete milestones, share resources, or grant rights as specified.

clauses.heading: 2. LLC Partnership Allocations
clauses.body: Recipient {member_name} is awarded a profit interest of {percentage_interest}% in LLC. Recipient shall share in LLC appreciation above the hurdle value of {hurdle_amount}.

clauses.heading: 3. Proprietary Information and Secrecy
clauses.body: Both parties agree to treat all financial metrics, corporate plans, cap tables, and research files received during the execution of this Agreement as confidential and proprietary.

clauses.heading: 4. Governing Law
clauses.body: This Agreement shall be governed and interpreted under the laws of the State/Country of {governing_state}.

faq.question: What is a distribution hurdle?
faq.answer: It ensures the recipient only profits from the value created AFTER the date of their grant, preventing them from taking historical value.

faq.question: Is a profit interest taxed upon grant?
faq.answer: If drafted properly (meeting IRS Safe Harbor rules), the grant is non-taxable, and gains are treated as capital gains rather than W2 income.
